name: Sierra de Puertollano and Huertezuelas de Calatrava Loop
name_original: Sierra de Puertollano y Huertezuelas de Calatrava — circular desde Calzada de Calatrava
description: Challenging 83.2-mile road cycling loop in Sierra de Puertollano y Huertezuelas de Calatrava with 5591 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Road cycling around Calzada De Calatrava features a diverse landscape, ranging from open plains to river valleys and more challenging hilly terrain. The region is characterized by its agricultural fields, the presence of the Río Jabalón, and the Montoro reservoir, offering varied scenery for cyclists. Routes often include gentle gradients along riverbanks and more significant climbs when traversing areas like the Sierra de Puertollano. This mix provides options for different fitness levels, from moderate rides through the Campo de Calatrava to difficult ascents. Best road cycling routes around Calzada De Calatrava The most popular road cycling route is Puerto Madrona y Puerto de los Rehoyos — circular por el Pantano del Montoro, a 68.4 miles (110.0 km) trail that takes 6 hours 5 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain and views of the Montoro reservoir. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is From Aldea del Rey to Pozuelo de Calatrava — circular by the Río Jabalón, a moderate 33.8 miles (54.4 km) path. This route follows the Río Jabalón, offering a more gentle cycling experience through the river valley. Local road cyclists also love the Campo de Calatrava Loop, a 56.0 miles (90.1 km) trail leading through the open landscapes of the Campo de Calatrava, often completed in about 3 hours 45 minutes. Road cycling around Calzada De Calatrava is defined by open plains, river valleys, and rolling hills, with routes traversing agricultural land and natural areas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to challenging climbs. The routes in Calzada De Calatrava are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.3 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 100 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Calzada De Calatrava's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Calzada De Calatrava?
A: There are 7 road cycling routes documented on komoot for the Calzada De Calatrava region. These routes cater to various skill levels, including 1 easy, 3 moderate, and 3 difficult options.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Calzada De Calatrava?
A: The region offers a diverse landscape for road cycling, from open plains and agricultural fields in areas like the Campo de Calatrava to river valleys along the Río Jabalón, and more challenging hilly terrain when traversing areas such as the Sierra de Puertollano. You'll find both gentle gradients and significant climbs.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Calzada De Calatrava?
A: Yes, there is at least one easy road cycling route available. While specific details for an easy route aren't provided, the region's river valleys often offer more gentle cycling experiences suitable for beginners.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1249475549/puerto-madrona-y-puerto-de-los-rehoyos-circular-por-el-pantano-del-montoro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto Madrona y Puerto de los Rehoyos — circular por el Pantano del Montoro&lt;/a&gt;, which is 110 km long with significant elevation gain. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1246843298/sierra-de-puertollano-and-huertezuelas-de-calatrava-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Puertollano and Huertezuelas de Calatrava Loop&lt;/a&gt;, spanning over 133 km.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in Calzada De Calatrava are designed as loops. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1251868469/campo-de-calatrava-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campo de Calatrava Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 90 km moderate ride, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1249631234/from-aldea-del-rey-to-pozuelo-de-calatrava-circular-by-the-rio-jabalon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Aldea del Rey to Pozuelo de Calatrava — circular by the Río Jabalón&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 54 km route.

Q: What can I see along the road cycling routes in Calzada De Calatrava?
A: Road cycling routes often pass by significant landmarks and natural features. You might encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2713619" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Calatrava la Nueva and Sacred Convent&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2713622" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oreto and Zuqueca Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt;. Natural attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3793255" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hervideros Baños del Barranco&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2713621" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vega del Jabalón Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best season for road biking in Calzada De Calatrava?
A: While specific seasonal recommendations are not provided, the region's climate suggests that spring and autumn would likely offer the most comfortable temperatures for road cycling. Summers can be very hot, especially in open plains, so early morning or late afternoon rides would be advisable during those months.

Q: Are there any routes that follow river valleys?
A: Yes, the region features the Río Jabalón, and routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1249631234/from-aldea-del-rey-to-pozuelo-de-calatrava-circular-by-the-rio-jabalon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Aldea del Rey to Pozuelo de Calatrava — circular by the Río Jabalón&lt;/a&gt; offer a more gentle cycling experience through the river valley, providing varied scenery.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Calzada De Calatrava?
A: The road cycling routes in Calzada De Calatrava are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.3 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 100 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, suggesting a generally positive experience.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling options?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer rides, routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1246843298/sierra-de-puertollano-and-huertezuelas-de-calatrava-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Puertollano and Huertezuelas de Calatrava Loop&lt;/a&gt; cover approximately 134 km, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1246876466/huertezuelas-viso-del-marques-y-santa-cruz-de-mudela-circular-desde-calzada-de-calatrava" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huertezuelas, Viso del Marqués y Santa Cruz de Mudela — circular desde Calzada de Calatrava&lt;/a&gt; is around 116 km, offering substantial distances for endurance cyclists.

Q: Can I find routes with views of reservoirs?
A: Absolutely. The region includes the Montoro reservoir, and routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1249475549/puerto-madrona-y-puerto-de-los-rehoyos-circular-por-el-pantano-del-montoro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto Madrona y Puerto de los Rehoyos — circular por el Pantano del Montoro&lt;/a&gt; feature views of this body of water, adding scenic beauty to your ride.
tips: You can see the fortified castle from miles away. You can understand the reasons why it was built on this dominant hill. Beautiful views of the defensive complex of the castle.
At the foot of the Sierra Madrona, Mestanza is a small town south of Puertollano, in the Alcudia Valley. Have a drink at the fountain in the main square before embarking on the climbs ahead.
Cycling up these spectacular mountains or cycling down to the valley, you will be able to contemplate incredible views of the Valle de Alcudia and Sierra Madrona Natural Park.

At 700 meters above sea level, the small town of Huertezuelas is a cozy place to rest while ascending from the river along the CR-5041 highway. Rest and drink some more water because it is not the end of the climb.
Shady area of the park where you can stop before the real climbing starts and fill up your jerry cans. Incredible views of the Fresneda river gorge.

It is unknown who and when it was founded, having news of its existence in 1579.
A very popular and highly devoted saint for being the lawyer against the black plague, there were a large number of hermitages in his name in Campo de Calatrava. It can be assured that together with the hermitage of El Salvador it is the oldest in the town, since at the end of the 15th century there was a brotherhood in his name, with a hermitage at the beginning of the 16th century.
Improvements and repairs were made to it over the centuries, a fact proven by the alms given by the citizens of the town in their wills in the books of the deceased. The hermitage is a nave 21 meters long by 5.40 meters wide, tiled floor and mortar and stone walls, with a sacristy measuring 5 by 3.21 meters.
It is located in the square of the same name, San Sebastián. Its cobbled square was also confiscated in 1806. Inside, three very old images are kept, which were saved from the destruction of July 1936, the owner of San Sebastián, Santa Bárbara (who resided for centuries in the hermitage of Ntra Mrs. de los Remedios) and San Antón (previously in the hermitage of the Holy Trinity).

Walking through its streets we will discover the town where Pedro Almodóvar spent his childhood. Just behind the park that bears his name is the old factory of the well-known Glue Imedio which, curiously, is also related to the cinema. In the 1930s, Gregorio Imedio ran the Cine Imedio, as celluloid usually broke due to use, Gregorio repaired the rolls at his parents' drugstore and that's how he discovered Imedio glue.

The current theater is located in what was the old summer cinema, where from the wall of his aunt's house, Almodóvar watched the first films of his childhood.

From a geological point of view, Calzada de Calatrava has numerous volcanic outcrops.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This challenging road cycling route is approximately 134 km long with over 1700 meters of elevation gain. It typically takes around 6 hours and 25 minutes to complete, depending on your fitness level and stops along the way.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as difficult due to its significant length and elevation gain. It requires very good fitness and is best suited for experienced road cyclists.

faqs.question: Where does the route start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route is circular and starts from Calzada de Calatrava. You can find parking facilities within Calzada de Calatrava, making it convenient to begin and end your ride in the town.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for road cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The route is predominantly on asphalt, with excellent road surfaces suitable for race bikes. While there are significant elevation changes, the paved nature of the trail ensures a generally smooth ride. There are minimal unpaved sections, less than 100 meters.

faqs.question: What historical landmarks or viewpoints can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers a blend of natural beauty and historical significance. You'll encounter breathtaking views such as the View of Castillo de Calatrava la Nueva and the View of Parque Natural Valle de Alcudia y Sierra Madrona. Other notable points include Mestanza Village, Huertezuelas, and the San Sebastian Hermitage (Calzada Cva), all set within a region rich in the history of the Order of Calatrava.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle in the Ciudad Real province, including this route, is typically during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). These seasons offer more moderate temperatures, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter. Be aware that open plains can be challenging due to wind, regardless of the season.

faqs.question: Does this route follow any official trails or Vías Verdes?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with several official trails. You will find yourself on parts of the [CIMA CR01] Collado Pisa, Camino a Fontanar, Palacio de Claveria. Castillo de Cva. la Nueva, Monumento Natural Carbonífero de Puertollano. Puertollano - Dehesa Boyal, and Cordel_alcoba.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for road cycling on paved surfaces. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, bringing a dog on a long, difficult road cycling route like this is not typically recommended for their safety or comfort, especially given the distance and elevation.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It traverses public roads and areas within the Parque Natural Valle de Alcudia y Sierra Madrona, which does not typically charge an entrance fee for general access.

faqs.question: Are there places to refill water or find refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through several settlements like Mestanza Village and Huertezuelas, where you can find amenities. Additionally, you'll pass by the Fuente de las Hoces del Río Fresneda, which can be a good spot for water. It's always advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer rides.

faqs.question: What should I be aware of regarding weather conditions on this route?
faqs.answer: The region can experience significant temperature variations. Summers can be very hot, while winters can be cold. The open plains of La Mancha, which this route traverses, can also be particularly exposed to strong winds, making some sections more challenging. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out and dress appropriately.
